metadata.title: AI Consumption in Context
metadata.subtitle: AI energy and water use compared with everyday activities, with measurement boundaries labelled
metadata.sources: IEA, LBNL, EPA, USGS, USDA, ASCE, GCSAA/USGA, NGF, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, Epoch AI, Hugging Face AI Energy Score, ML.Energy, Pew Research Center, PJM/Monitoring Analytics, Morgan Stanley, The Guardian/Cleanview, MIT Technology Review, Harvard/UCLA Study

waterConsumption.scaleNote: Bar lengths are drawn to scale (linear). The bottom three bars are shown at the minimum visible width - at true scale each would be thinner than a pixel, and ChatGPT's cooling-water bar would be less than 1/400,000th of the top bar.

items.label: <u>Global</u> Water Network Losses
items.notes: Extrapolated from utility-reported non-revenue water rates (~346 million cubic metres/day) - no one meters every country's losses. Frequently misattributed to the US alone; the US share is roughly 8.3 trillion litres - a reminder that a number's boundary matters as much as its size

items.label: US Corn Irrigation
items.notes: ~35-40% of US corn becomes ethanol; each litre of ethanol carries a ~1,200-1,500 litre water footprint (mostly evapotranspiration)

items.label: US Treated Water Lost to Leaks
items.notes: ~240,000 water main breaks per year; nearly 20% of US water mains have exceeded their useful life

items.label: US Household Leaks
items.notes: Equivalent to the water use of 11 million homes; the average family wastes 9,400 gallons (35,600 litres) a year

items.label: All <u>Global</u> AI Data Centres
items.source: de Vries (VU Amsterdam) / UC Riverside via IEEE Spectrum
items.notes: Wide uncertainty band; counts on-site cooling plus water consumed generating the electricity. Morgan Stanley projects ~1,068 billion L/yr by 2028 on this boundary

items.label: Google Data Centres + Offices (<u>Global</u>)
items.notes: 8.1 billion gallons consumed in 2024; ~9.9 billion gallons in 2025 (derived from Google's replenishment disclosure)

items.label: Microsoft (<u>Global</u>, Consumption)
items.notes: Consumption (evaporated), not withdrawal - the two are often conflated in coverage

items.label: ChatGPT Cooling Water (<u>Global</u>, Annual)
items.notes: 0.32 mL/query x 2.5B+ queries/day x 365. Excludes power-generation and chip-fab water; no methodology published. Corrects an arithmetic error in earlier versions of this page (111.2B)

chatgpt.notes: On-site cooling only; no methodology published

gemini.notes: About 5 drops of water; median text prompt, full-stack methodology

mistral.notes: Looks 170x higher than Google's figure because Mistral also counts water consumed generating the electricity (Scope 2) - the gap is methodology, not thirst

perQuery.comparison: A single dripping tap wastes 11,000+ litres/year - equivalent to 35 million ChatGPT queries

energyConsumption.scaleNote: Bar lengths are drawn to scale (linear). Bars under 1 Wh are shown at the minimum visible width - at true scale a ChatGPT query's bar would be about 1/2,900th of the Sora bar.

items.label: Google Gemini Query
items.notes: Median text prompt, full-stack methodology; still the latest official disclosure as of mid-2026

items.label: ChatGPT Query (average)
items.notes: No methodology published; independently corroborated by Epoch AI's ~0.3 Wh estimate for GPT-4o

items.label: Google Gemini Query (2024)
items.notes: Same query type, 12 months earlier - 33x less efficient (derived: 0.24 Wh x 33)

items.label: Google Search
items.source: Google (2009 figure - stale baseline, no modern disclosure)

items.label: Reasoning Query (complex)
items.notes: Epoch AI point estimate (~11x a standard query); measured open-model averages run higher at 25-30x (HF AI Energy Score v2, ML.Energy v3)

items.label: AI Image Generation
items.notes: 46x range across models; higher resolution adds 1.3-4.7x

items.label: Netflix Streaming (1 hour HD)
items.notes: ~72% of this is your TV/device, not the data centre

items.label: Sora 2 AI Video (10 seconds)
items.notes: ~2,900x a text query; derived estimate. Measured open video models span 0.14-109 Wh per clip - an 800x range

energyConsumption.uncertaintyNote: Energy per query ranges from 0.1-4 Wh for standard text depending on model, token count, and complexity. Reasoning modes average 25-30x more (measured); extreme cases reach 150-700x.

reasoningModels.title: Reasoning Models Changed the Maths

sources.name: Hugging Face AI Energy Score v2
sources.finding: Reasoning models average 30x more energy; same model with reasoning on vs off measured at 150-700x

sources.name: ML.Energy Leaderboard v3
sources.finding: Problem-solving (reasoning) responses used 25x more energy than conversation, driven by ~10x more output tokens at lower GPU utilisation

reasoningModels.disclosureGap: Only Google (Gemini: 0.24 Wh, 0.26 mL, 0.03 g CO2e per median prompt) and Mistral (Large 2: 1.14 g CO2e, 45 mL per 400-token reply, Scope 2 included) publish audited per-model figures. OpenAI offers an aggregate CEO figure with no methodology; Anthropic, xAI and Meta publish nothing per-query as of mid-2026. Anthropic declined environmental disclosure in Stanford's Dec 2025 transparency index and has no sustainability report, though it joined the Frontier carbon-removal coalition (Jun 2026) and is hiring emissions-reporting staff.
reasoningModels.newerNotGreener: Newer models are not automatically more efficient: 8 of 14 comparable models used equal or more energy than their early-2025 predecessors (HF AI Energy Score v2). MoE architectures help - active-parameter count drives energy, with a measured 3.6x lower energy/token vs a similar-size dense model.

boundaries.title: Why the Water Numbers Disagree
boundaries.explainer: Resource figures depend on where the measurement boundary sits. Small numbers count only on-site cooling at query time; large numbers add the water consumed generating the electricity (Scope 2), chip fabrication (Scope 3), and projections of growth. Both can be arithmetically honest.

small.boundary: On-site cooling only, per query, today

large.boundary: All AI data centres, cooling + power-generation + chip-fab water, projected 2028 (~11x 2024)

boundaries.withdrawalVsConsumption: US thermoelectric power plants account for 41% of total water withdrawals (34% of freshwater withdrawals, USGS) - but only ~3-4% of that is consumed (evaporated); the rest returns to the source. Counting withdrawals as 'use' inflates any electricity-linked water figure ~25x.

locality.fact: ~2/3 of new US data centres built since 2022 are in high water-stress areas
locality.plannedBuilds: 517 of 809 planned US data centres are on land that was in drought throughout the preceding year - developers favour arid regions for cheap land, tax breaks and less equipment corrosion. A large site can draw up to 5 million gallons (~19 million litres) a day, the water use of a town of up to 50,000 people
locality.plannedBuildsSource: Guardian analysis of Cleanview and US Drought Monitor data, June 2026
locality.note: Water is local: the same facility can be benign in a wet region and a real problem in a dry one. Quantity matters less than where it is drawn.
locality.operatorResponse: Sam Altman said in Feb 2026 that OpenAI's data centres have largely moved off evaporative cooling (CNBC); Project Rainier, the 2.2 GW AWS campus that trains Claude, is air-cooled ~98% of the year at an estimated PUE ~1.15, though AWS won't quantify operational water (MeasuredAI, from permits and utility filings); facility-level water data remains largely undisclosed (AGU Advances, 2026)

videoVsText.title: Video Generation: The Real Energy Consumer
videoVsText.ratio: ~2,900x more energy for a 10-second video vs a text query
videoVsText.insight: Concerns about AI energy should distinguish between text (minimal), reasoning modes (25-30x text), and video generation (thousands of times text)

efficiencyImprovements.title: AI Efficiency is Improving - With Caveats

google.source: Google (self-reported, no independent verification as of mid-2026)

costPerToken.finding: LLM inference price per token fell ~50x/year (median across benchmarks; ~200x/year after Jan 2024), expected to decelerate to 3-5x/year through 2027

hardware.finding: NVIDIA markets Blackwell as 10-50x more efficient than Hopper; independent teardown puts the real per-watt silicon gain at ~47%, with the rest from FP4 quantisation and architecture changes

efficiencyImprovements.caveat: Efficiency per token improves rapidly, but reasoning models spend ~10x more tokens per query - so energy per QUERY can rise even as energy per token falls. 8 of 14 comparable models used equal or more energy than their early-2025 predecessors (HF AI Energy Score v2).

potentialOptimisation.source: University of Michigan (2023 study, still cited)
potentialOptimisation.method: Tuning GPU power limits, batch size, and location selection

moe.description: Mixture-of-experts models: active-parameter count drives energy, not total - measured 3.6x lower energy/token vs similar-size dense model (ML.Energy v3)

dataCentreIndustry.title: The Bigger Picture: Data Centre Industry

usEnergyShare.unit: % of US electricity (2023, latest full-economy data)
usEnergyShare.source: LBNL 2024 US Data Center Energy Usage Report

global2025.growth: +17% year on year; AI-focused data centres +50%

usEmissions.source: Harvard/UCLA Study (2023 data; hyperscale subset independently reproduced June 2026)

carbonIntensity.reason: 95% of US data centres in locations with dirtier-than-average electricity due to 24/7 power needs
carbonIntensity.source: Harvard/UCLA Study; independently reproduced (~545 g) by June 2026 hyperscale study

regionalVariation.variationFactor: 16x difference between best and worst locations

electricityPrices.title: The 2026 story: electricity prices
electricityPrices.pjmRecord: $333.44/MW-day record capacity price (2027/28 auction, third consecutive record); $16.4B total market cost
electricityPrices.attribution: PJM's market monitor attributes 63% of the 2025/26 auction price increase ($9.3B recovered from customers) to data centres
electricityPrices.billImpact: Residential bills in PJM states rising 1.5-5% from June 2026 (~$16-18/month in the worst-hit areas); NRDC projects up to $163B in PJM bill increases through 2033 (model-dependent scenario)
electricityPrices.sources: PJM, Monitoring Analytics, Canary Media, IEEFA (Dec 2025 - 2026)

iea2030.source: IEA Electricity 2026; central case unchanged, higher scenarios now judged less likely due to supply-chain bottlenecks

fossilFuelShare.unit: % of new demand met by fossil fuels by 2030
fossilFuelShare.source: Goldman Sachs; consistent with 2026 gas-turbine order books (~100 GW vs 60-70 GW/yr manufacturing capacity)

dataCentreIndustry.overbuildRisk: The buildout may not materialise as promised: Microsoft froze ~1.5 GW of self-build capacity and Satya Nadella conceded 'there will be an overbuild'; the Bank for International Settlements lists an AI capex bust among its top systemic risks (2026), noting ~$1T/year hyperscaler capex against ~$50-60B AI revenue
dataCentreIndustry.context: Data centres are ~8% of projected global electricity demand GROWTH to 2030 - less than EVs or air conditioning (Carbon Brief). Current scale is modest; the trajectory and its local grid/water impacts are the legitimate concerns.

items.label: Average US Car Commute
items.notes: 69.2% of Americans drive alone to work (ACS 2024)

context.totalNational: $90+ billion spent annually on commuting fuel

models.label: GPT-4 Training
equivalents: ~300 round-trip flights NYC to San Francisco
models.notes: Training cost over $100 million; ~25,000 A100 GPUs for 90-100 days

models.label: Grok 4 Training
models.notes: Largest credibly-estimated training run: ~6x GPT-4; 246M H100-hours, ~$490M compute

models.label: GPT-5 Training
models.notes: Used LESS training compute than GPT-4.5 - OpenAI scaled post-training on a smaller base model. Frontier training is not monotonically growing; no credible GWh estimate exists

aiTraining.trend: Frontier training runs now draw >100 MW; training power demand grows ~2.2x/year while hardware efficiency improves ~40%/year (Epoch AI). GW-scale announcements (Stargate, Colossus 2) are build-out CAPACITY targets, not measured consumption.
aiTraining.context: Training is a one-time cost amortised across all users. Over a deployed model's lifetime, INFERENCE dominates: estimates range 60-90% of total footprint depending on boundary.

annualEnergy.source: Derived: 0.34 Wh x 2.5B/day x 365; MIT Technology Review independently estimated ~300 GWh

monthlyActive.source: Reuters / Sensor Tower estimate (MAU, a different metric to WAU)

usa.trafficShare: ~17-19% of global usage (US); ~19.8% for North America (OpenAI)

adoption.platformShare: ChatGPT 44%, Gemini 24%, Copilot 17%, Meta AI 14%, Grok 8%, Claude 6%
adoption.source: Pew Research Center (survey Feb 2026, n=5,119, published Jun 2026)

individualImpact.title: Personal AI Usage in Context

dailyUsage.context: Less than running a fridge for 2 minutes

individualImpact.source: Derived from EIA average household consumption (~10,500 kWh/yr)
individualImpact.note: Even 100 standard queries/day is ~0.12% of typical household electricity. Corrects the 0.3% previously shown. Reasoning-heavy use runs higher (~25-30x per query)

dailyHabitsComparison.title: Typical Daily Usage Patterns
dailyHabitsComparison.description: How energy consumption compares for average daily use

sources: eMarketer US Time Spent With Media 2025
tvStreaming.notes: 2025 reported as the peak year; traditional TV component now declining

chatgpt.methodology: 2.5B+ daily prompts / 900M weekly active users ≈ 2.8 prompts/day

comparison.description: TV/streaming uses ~550x more energy daily than typical ChatGPT usage

perspectives.title: Household Leaks
perspectives.fact: A single dripping tap wastes 11,000+ litres per year
perspectives.context: EPA estimates 10% of US homes have leaks wasting 340+ litres daily

perspectives.title: Daily Media Consumption
perspectives.fact: Average American watches 6 hours 45 minutes of TV/streaming per day
perspectives.comparison: Uses 520 Wh daily vs ~1 Wh for typical AI use (2-3 queries)

perspectives.title: Streaming Energy
perspectives.fact: 1 hour of Netflix streaming uses 77 Wh
perspectives.comparison: Equivalent to 227 ChatGPT queries - and ~72% of it is your TV, not the data centre
perspectives.context: Video streaming accounts for ~60% of global internet traffic

perspectives.title: Golf Course Water Use
perspectives.fact: US golf courses use 2.01 trillion litres of water per year
perspectives.comparison: Roughly 3-6x ALL global AI data centres combined - including the water behind their electricity
perspectives.context: ~16,000 courses serving 29.1 million golfers vs 900+ million ChatGPT users

perspectives.title: Corn and Ethanol
perspectives.fact: US corn irrigation draws ~20.4 trillion litres per year - and ~35-40% of the crop is burned as car fuel
perspectives.comparison: 26-65x the water footprint of all global AI data centres
perspectives.context: Each litre of ethanol carries a ~1,200-1,500 litre water footprint (USDA/Water Resources Research). Different water kinds: irrigation is largely non-potable; data centre cooling is often municipal

perspectives.title: Same Industry, 170x Apart
perspectives.fact: Mistral discloses 45 mL of water per reply; Google discloses 0.26 mL per prompt
perspectives.comparison: The 170x gap is methodology, not thirst
perspectives.context: Mistral counts water consumed generating the electricity (Scope 2); Google counts on-site cooling. Never compare figures without checking the boundary

perspectives.title: Water is Local
perspectives.fact: ~2/3 of new US data centres since 2022 are in high water-stress areas
perspectives.comparison: The same facility can be benign in a wet region and a problem in a dry one
perspectives.context: Bloomberg / S&P Global; a Jun 2026 Guardian analysis found 517 of 809 planned US facilities on drought-hit land. Facility-level water data remains largely undisclosed

perspectives.title: Reasoning Costs Tokens
perspectives.fact: Reasoning modes use 25-30x more energy than standard queries on average
perspectives.comparison: Extreme cases measured at 150-700x, driven by ~10x more output tokens
perspectives.context: Measured: Hugging Face AI Energy Score v2, ML.Energy v3 (Dec 2025). One visible query can be many hidden ones

perspectives.title: Electricity Prices
perspectives.fact: Data centres drove 63% of the record PJM capacity price increase - $9.3B recovered from customers
perspectives.comparison: Residential bills in PJM states rising 1.5-5% from June 2026
perspectives.context: PJM market monitor / Canary Media. The strongest evidence yet of AI's cost to ordinary bill-payers

perspectives.title: Commuting Emissions
perspectives.fact: One year of average US car commuting uses 1,066 litres of fuel
perspectives.context: 69% of Americans drive alone to work

perspectives.title: Claude's Undisclosed Footprint
perspectives.fact: Anthropic holds ~40% of enterprise LLM API spend but publishes no query volume or per-query figures
perspectives.comparison: Only 6% consumer reach, yet the largest enterprise share - an API-shaped footprint
perspectives.context: Menlo Ventures (year-end 2025); Pew (Feb 2026). With no disclosed volume or per-query data, Claude's total footprint can't be independently estimated

keyInsights.description: More energy used by reasoning modes vs standard queries (measured)
ChatGPT queries to equal 1 hour of Netflix streaming
More energy for daily TV/streaming than typical daily ChatGPT use
More water used by US golf courses than ALL global AI data centres
US adults who have used AI chatbots (Pew, Feb 2026)

mythBusters.reality: US data centres are 2.18% of national emissions (2023 data) - comparable to aviation. The concern is the trajectory: global data centre electricity grew 17% in 2025, with AI-focused facilities up ~50%.

mythBusters.myth: Every AI query wastes massive resources
mythBusters.reality: A typical text query uses 0.24-0.34 Wh and ~5 drops of water - like microwaving for 1 second. Reasoning modes (25-30x) and video generation (~2,900x) are the real outliers.
mythBusters.source: Google, OpenAI, HF AI Energy Score

mythBusters.myth: Each query uses a bottle of water
mythBusters.reality: Official on-site figures are 0.26-0.32 mL - about 5 drops. The viral ~500 mL figure counted the water behind electricity generation for older models. Boundary, not dishonesty, explains the 1,000x gap.
Inference cost per token fell ~50x/year (Epoch AI) and Google reports a 33x per-query gain. But reasoning models spend ~10x more tokens, so energy per query can still rise.

mythBusters.myth: Data centres run on clean energy
mythBusters.reality: 95% of US data centres sit on dirtier-than-average grids (48% above US average carbon intensity, independently reproduced in 2026), ~2/3 of new builds are in water-stressed areas, and the near-term buildout is gas-heavy.

mythBusters.myth: Training is the main energy cost
mythBusters.reality: Over a model's lifetime, inference dominates (60-90% of footprint). And frontier training isn't monotonically growing: GPT-5 used less training compute than GPT-4.5.

mythBusters.myth: The water numbers must be lies - they differ by 1,000x
mythBusters.reality: Altman's 0.32 mL counts on-site cooling per query; Morgan Stanley's ~1 trillion litres by 2028 adds power-generation and chip-fab water, projected forward. Both can be arithmetically honest. Always ask where the boundary sits.

quotes.text: Individual usage of ChatGPT and other LLMs for most people is a small part of their carbon and energy footprint.
We should stop trying to reverse-engineer numbers based on hearsay and put more pressure on these companies to actually share the real ones.
quotes.author: Sasha Luccioni

quotes.text: It suggests that energy demand for data centres and AI will still be pretty small for the next five years at least. I read it as them saying: 'Everyone just needs to chill out a bit.'
quotes.author: Hannah Ritchie
quotes.role: Data scientist, Our World in Data (on the IEA outlook)

quotes.text: Disappointment in returns could trigger a sudden pullback in financing and turn the capex boom into a protracted investment bust.
quotes.author: Bank for International Settlements

credits.framing: The measurement-boundary framing of the water section was prompted by Hank Green's video 'Why is Everyone So Wrong About AI Water Use?' (June 2026). Figures were independently verified against primary sources; where his numbers didn't hold (corn total, the 'training is 50%' claim), the verified figures are used instead.
